summary: Dependency-confusion canary exfiltrates host and CI identifiers on install
details: Package published to the public npm registry under the `@luminarycloudinternal` scope at version `9999.0.2` — the dependency-confusion version-bypass shape targeting a private org namespace. On install, `postinstall.js` runs an HTTPS GET to `https://poc-luminary-npm-1782987043.testingboxes.com/cb/...` carrying `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, OS type/release/arch, and GitHub Actions identifiers (`GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `GITHUB_SHA`, `GITHUB_REF`, `GITHUB_WORKFLOW`, `RUNNER_NAME`, `CI`), plus the package name/version. Any build that misresolves the private `@luminarycloudinternal` scope to the public registry will fire the beacon unconditionally, leaking internal repository names, commit SHAs, workflow names, and runner identity from private CI to a third-party host. The self-description as a bug-bounty canary does not change the behavior at the installer: private CI metadata is transmitted without consent to a non-first-party endpoint at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
